nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Thorpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): from Middle English thorp(e) ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English thorp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from any of several places called Thorp(e), which are most frequent in Yorkshire, the Midlands, and East Anglia. Compare Throop .

variants.name: Trupp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Hermann, Reinhold.

English (Herefordshire and Monmouthshire): variant of   Thorpe  , or from a place so named, such as Thrupp (Berkshire, Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire), Thrupe (Somerset), Throop (Dorset, Hampshire, Somerset), and Throope (Wiltshire).

variants.name: Throop
meanings: English: from Middle English throp ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English throp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from Throop in Hampshire, Throope in Wiltshire, Thrup in Oxfordshire, or any of the places called Thrupp in Berkshire, Gloucestershire, and Northamptonshire. Compare Thorpe .

variants.name: Tharpe
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Thorpe  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Thorp
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Thorpe  .

variants.name: Troup
meanings: Scottish (eastern): habitational name from a place in the parish of Gamrie, near Banff. The placename is unexplained, perhaps a Pictish cognate of Welsh trwp ‘vessel, dish, basin’.
Possibly also English, a metathesized form of   Thorpe  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret De Thorpe was born in 1401, in Hessett, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Edmund Thorpe, was 59 and her mother, Joan de Northwode, was 43. She had at least 3 sons and 4 daughters with Stephen Bacon. She died on 11 October 1443, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret De Thorpe est née en 1401, à Hessett, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edmund Thorpe, avait 59 ans et sa mère, Joan de Northwode, avait 43 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles avec Stephen Bacon. Elle est décédée le 11 octobre 1443, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret De Thorpe 1401 in Hessett, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund Thorpe 59 und ihre Mutter Joan de Northwode 43. Sie hatte mit Stephen Bacon mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 11. Oktober 1443 im Alter von 42 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret De Thorpe è nata il nel 1401 nel Hessett, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Edmund Thorpe, aveva 59 anni e sua madre, Joan de Northwode, aveva 43 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie con Stephen Bacon. Morì il 11 ottobre 1443, all'età di 42 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpe(이)가 1401년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 헤싯에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Thorpe(은)는 59세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Northwode(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 Stephen Bacon(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1443년 10월 11일 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret De Thorpe nasceu em 1401, em Hessett, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Edmund Thorpe, tinha 59 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Northwode, tinha 43 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas com Stephen Bacon. Ela faleceu em 11 de outubro de 1443, com 42 anos.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret De Thorpe nació en 1401, en Hessett, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Edmund Thorpe, tenía 59 años y su mamá, Joan de Northwode, tenía 43 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas con Stephen Bacon. Murió el 11 de octubre de 1443, a la edad de 42 años.
